What's going on everyone? How to maximize ROI of this program beyond what I just gave you in the last few videos. So here's basically how you can utilize support and there's kind of two components to support. You have technical support which is software, ads, platforms, you know, the more technical things that you need to do on the computer and then you have the more strategic side which is sales, managing employees, you know, commission structures, how to lay out the workouts, things like that. So like kind of more gym specific strategy versus like technical marketing, etc. Okay, so I'm going to talk first about the technical marketing help which is what this slide is for. If you are using support, right, when you use support, support is for helping you get unstuck. Okay, what it is not for is doing it for you. So if you're like, well I haven't really started, I was just hoping you could walk me through it, that's what the videos are for. You will not get like, if we wanted to do it on our own for gym owners, so basically giving people fish instead of teaching them to fish, we would have an entirely different business structure and it's honestly a lot easier because it takes us 90 minutes to do everything in gym launch from zero to live, right? Like if you know what you're doing, it does not take a long time. But for you to acquire the skill of learning how to fish for yourself, learning how to make it rain for yourself, you need to do it. Like that is the value here. That is what you are paying us, that is the product, right? Is for you to understand how to make rain for your business and never need a company ever again, that's the point, okay? So if you get stuck, that is when you reach out for support. Make sure you watch the video, like because other people have gotten through it by watching the video. Sometimes you just have to watch it again, follow along, etc. It is not for like throwing up your hands and just having someone else do it for you because that is a victim mindset and we will never be for that, alright? Cool. So the Facebook group is where you should be reaching out for strategic help. So help on sales, help on show rates, help on anything that has to do that's not technical, right? So technical stuff. Support which is through intercom which I will get to in a second. But the Facebook group is where you ask those questions and mind you, be wise when you post your questions and what I mean by that is like, don't sound like a negative Nancy. So be a nice person, right? Say like, hey, like I am doing X, Y and Z, I know I am struggling with this. Is anyone here, anyone else here struggle with that and like what should I do, etc. Like ask the question, don't say for example, this sucks, like everyone is terrible, I hate, blah, blah, blah, like it's just don't be a victim, right? Like ask questions but ask them politely, okay? Cool. That way it just keeps the group a lot nicer. Stats. So when you do ask questions, like I was just referencing, you need to ask them in this format, okay? The reason we ask you to ask them in this format is because we have the data to be able to help you because ultimately all of this is a pipeline. So a lot of times people are like, I have a sales problem but then they don't report what percentage of people they have met with and closed. And a lot of times you don't have a sales problem, you have a show problem or you don't have a show problem, you have a schedule problem or you don't have any problem, you have a lead flow problem. Whatever it is, we need the entire set of data so that we can give you a contextual answer that is valuable. If you say for example, I suck at sales, help, that is not, like you will not get good responses to that question because the answers will lack context. So this is how you report your data. I've been running this for, you know, I'm in week two, I've been running this for three days which hopefully don't ask for feedback in three days, like give it a week and a half before you start asking for it. Like you got to give it a minute. But anyways, I'm like, I'm four weeks in, I've been running this for, you know, 21 days, I've spent, you know, $2,000 on ads, we have 500 leads, we have 350 scheduled, of the 350 that's scheduled, we had 200 show, the 200 show that we closed 100 at X price point. So we brought in 50 grand, I have Facebook radius of 60,000 people in my seven mile radius, I'm doing all the calls and meetings myself. Right. From there, we will be able to spot what the deficit is, what the leak in the pipeline is so that we can help you. All right. And the same thing goes for the other gym owners in that community. Like everyone wants you to win. And so if you post it this way, we can help you win. Okay. If you have a question that you need more, more depth answers for, we have a daily call. And so we started this because I hated every other company that I had worked with. I was like, yeah, we do a monthly coaching call. It's like, well, by the time the monthly coaching call comes around or on every other week coaching call comes around, like my problem's already solved or like I've moved on from it, right? Or it's no longer relevant. And so I implemented the daily call at the very beginning and we only had like five clients and it has been something that has stuck ever since. And so now we have hundreds of people on the call who come in every day. And what I can tell you is that the people who tune in to that call every day are successful. It's all I can tell you. If you tune into that call every day, you will pick up something every single day that will help you make more money. And that will be from the other gyms, the questions that are answered, the coaches that we have. And the coaches that we have who are on there are all top 1% gyms and legacy. So these are guys who, or girls, who have executed everything legacy, have outsourced their gym entirely and are making a sizable profit month over month and implemented every single one of the steps that we have. So they will be able to help you because they have struggled and gone through the things that you are going through. Awesome. Intercom is going to be the little chat box that's in the bottom right hand corner when you log into the portal. You should be able to see it right now. It's in the bottom right. Yeah, right there. And just click there. And we have a five minute promise at the time of this making. We continue to improve that. And the hours, I won't say because we changed them, should be below. But we have a wide range of hours that we accommodate. I remember the story that I had a friend who's in the internet marketing space and he's like, how do you get around people expecting you to respond to an email within like an hour? Like it's kind of ridiculous that people think that. Like how do you get around that? And I was like, well, we responded in less than five minutes. And he was like, oh. And so this is something that we've pioneered. I don't know anyone else who's been doing this as part of the reason that a lot of the gyms that we have had super high success. We don't want you to be stuck. We don't want you to be stuck long if you do get stuck. So message Intercom and they will help you point in the right direction and help you get unstuck. They do this all the time. They're amazing. And we're very grateful for them. Billing. If you have billing questions, just go below this video. And then the last thing here is at week six-ish. I say ish because some people take a little longer to get started than others. But usually around week six, we will have one of our legacy coordinators reach out to you and do a mandatory call. And basically that's our way of basically helping you succeed, seeing where we can provide value. And then ultimately, if it does seem like a good fit, we talk about legacy. And legacy is kind of the main point of everything. Production launch is really just getting you started. Legacy is like how we can help you build up to $100,000 a month plus gym. And so that's, we just show you step by step how we do it and how we've had lots and lots of gyms who have followed that process. So it's far deeper than just how to get people in the door, but how to maximally profit, how to run your team, how to structure the business to make you as much money with as little work as possible. So anyways, I won't get beyond myself there. But this is how you access support. It is for you getting unstuck. It is not for doing it for you on the technical side.
